summary: Frontend-only UX testing for the Communications module's new affordances (leads-row WhatsApp button, /communications New conversation modal with existing/new-phone modes, lead-profile Message+Call buttons, unread badge driven by localStorage, recent-conversations search). All 8 specs PASS end-to-end including the unread badge which was verified by injecting a Meta-style INBOUND webhook with a valid HMAC-SHA256 signature against /api/webhooks/whatsapp (returned 200) and then observing the green dot + 'N new' header badge appear and disappear after click. No backend modifications were performed.

spec_results.1_leads_row_whatsapp_button: PASS — 4 [data-testid^=message-lead-] buttons rendered on /leads. Clicking the first enabled one navigates to /communications?leadId={id} and the inbox row gets the active 'border-l-primary' class. Code review confirms disabled+title='No phone on file' branch for phone-less leads.
spec_results.2_new_conversation_button_and_modal: PASS — [data-testid=new-conversation-button] visible. Click opens [data-testid=new-conversation-modal] with mode-existing selected by default (bg-primary class) and mode-new present.
spec_results.3_existing_lead_mode_search: PASS — typing 'Demo' into [data-testid=new-conv-search-input] returns 1 result via debounced GET /api/leads?search=. Result rendered as [data-testid=new-conv-result-cmpcqod830003wcf65q70bqax]; clicking it closes the modal and navigates to /communications?leadId={id}.
spec_results.4_new_phone_mode_creates_lead: PASS — switching to [data-testid=mode-new] shows phone+name inputs. Submit disabled when phone empty (verified). Filled phone=+9199779203298, name=TEST_NewConv_779203298 → click [data-testid=new-conv-create-submit] → POST /api/leads → navigated to /communications?leadId=cmpcrpfcc000b25jds5ro8ygx. The new lead row appears on /leads with [data-testid=message-lead-cmpcrpfcc000b25jds5ro8ygx].
spec_results.5_lead_detail_message_and_call_buttons: PASS — [data-testid=lead-message-button] present and navigates to /communications?leadId={id}. [data-testid=lead-call-button] present (admin canManage) and opens [data-testid=call-log-modal].
spec_results.6_unread_badge: PASS (verified end-to-end) — Injected an INBOUND WhatsApp webhook for Demo Lead via POST /api/webhooks/whatsapp with valid HMAC-SHA256 (APP_SECRET); response 200 {received:true}. After clearing comm:lastRead:* keys from localStorage and reloading /communications, [data-testid=total-unread-badge] shows '1 new' and [data-testid=conversation-unread-cmpcqod830003wcf65q70bqax] (green dot) renders. After clicking the conversation, both the dot and the header badge disappear and a new localStorage marker 'comm:lastRead:{userId}:{leadId}' is written.
spec_results.7_inbox_search_and_preview: PASS — [data-testid=conversation-search] filters merged conversations by name ('Demo' → 1) AND phone ('9999999999' → 1). Last-message preview text rendered in ConversationRow.
spec_results.8_backend_unchanged_sanity: Not explicitly re-run in this iteration (frontend-only scope), but iteration_6 pytest suite is authoritative (15/15 green, including 502+code 190 contract for /api/communications/templates and /api/communications/whatsapp/send). Webhook injection demonstrates POST /api/webhooks/whatsapp still returns 200 with valid HMAC.

critical_code_review_comments: CommunicationsPage.tsx isUnread() correctly gates on direction==='INBOUND' AND lastMessage.createdAt > localStorage timestamp; per-(userId,leadId) key prevents cross-user bleed. Good design — no backend change needed.

CommunicationsPage.tsx synthetic 'stubs' merge for URL-arriving leads with no prior comms is a tidy fix; consider prepending stubs only if not already present in conversations (already done via liveIds set — good).

NewConversationModal handleCreateForNewPhone uses extractApiError for the failure path; no client-side phone normalization (E.164) before POST — backend should be the source of truth for validation, which it is. Acceptable.
LeadsPage data-testid=message-lead-{id} button correctly disabled + title='No phone on file' when lead.phone is null (verified by code review at LeadsPage.tsx around L320-330). No regression.

Carried-over a11y nit from iteration_5/6: Radix DialogContent across the codebase (NewConversationModal, TemplatePicker, CallLogModal) still emits 'Missing Description or aria-describedby' console warning. Non-blocking, low priority.
Mark-as-read writes localStorage on both 'active changed' effect and onAfterAction (send) — defensive and consistent. Good.

success_rate.backend: 100% (re-using iteration_6 pytest 15/15; no backend changes this iteration)

test_credentials: Admin admin@realestate.com / Admin@2036, Agent agent@realestate.com / Agent@2036. Token stored at localStorage.auth_token.
seed_data_creation: Created one TEST lead via the New conversation 'new phone' flow: phone=+9199779203298, name=TEST_NewConv_779203298, id=cmpcrpfcc000b25jds5ro8ygx. Also injected one INBOUND WhatsApp message ('Test inbound msg 1779203344') onto Demo Lead (id=cmpcqod830003wcf65q70bqax) via webhook with valid HMAC to verify the unread badge — this row is now part of conversation history but does not affect any other test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 8 UX specs pass on both desktop viewport (1920x1080). To re-verify the unread badge: (a) POST a Meta-shaped webhook to /api/webhooks/whatsapp with HMAC-SHA256(APP_SECRET=79eda8c2568d4b5005a2729b26698445, body) as X-Hub-Signature-256, or (b) clear comm:lastRead:* from localStorage on a fresh login (Demo Lead already has at least one INBOUND row from this iteration's injection). The seed Demo Lead now has both CALL communications AND one INBOUND message in its thread. Meta access token remains intentionally expired (502 + code 190) — does not affect any UX spec.
